absolute_maximum_ratings.parameter: Voltage difference Thermal pad to GND

absolute_maximum_ratings.conditions: per JEDEC specification JESD22-C101, all pins

electrical_characteristics.conditions: No voltage hysteresis, rising and falling
No voltage hysteresis, rising and falling
-2 µA < ICOMP < 2 µA, VCOMP = 1 V, VVSENSE = 0.4 V

electrical_characteristics.parameter: RT/CLK falling edge to PH rising edge delay
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: RT/CLK falling edge to PH rising edge delay
electrical_characteristics.conditions: Measured at 500 kHz with RT resistor in series

pin_definitions.description: A bootstrap capacitor is required between BOOT and PH. If the voltage on this capacitor is below the minimum required by the output device, the output is forced to switch off until the capacitor is refreshed.
Input supply voltage, 3.5 V to 42 V.
Enable pin, internal pullup current source. Pull below 1.2 V to disable. Float to enable. Adjust the input undervoltage lockout with two resistors.

Slow-start and tracking. An external capacitor connected to this pin sets the output rise time. Because the voltage on this pin overrides the internal reference, it can be used for tracking and sequencing.

Resistor timing and external clock. An internal amplifier holds this pin at a fixed voltage when using an external resistor to ground to set the switching frequency. If the pin is pulled above the PLL upper threshold, a mode change occurs and the pin becomes a synchronization input. The internal amplifier is disabled and the pin is a high-impedance clock input to the internal PLL. If clocking edges stop, the internal amplifier is re-enabled and the mode returns to a resistor-set function.
An open-drain output; asserts low if output voltage is low due to thermal shutdown, dropout, overvoltage or EN shutdown.
Inverting node of the transconductance (gm) error amplifier.
Error amplifier output, and input to the output switch current comparator. Connect frequency compensation components to this pin.
The source of the internal high-side power MOSFET
GND pin must be electrically connected to the thermal pad on the printed circuit board for proper operation.

logical_pins.name: BOOT
logical_pins.description: A bootstrap capacitor is required between BOOT and PH. If the voltage on this capacitor is below the minimum required by the output device, the output is forced to switch off until the capacitor is refreshed.

logical_pins.description: Input supply voltage, 3.5 V to 42 V.

logical_pins.name: EN
logical_pins.description: Enable pin, internal pullup current source. Pull below 1.2 V to disable. Float to enable. Adjust the input undervoltage lockout with two resistors.

logical_pins.name: SS/TR
logical_pins.description: Slow-start and tracking. An external capacitor connected to this pin sets the output rise time. Because the voltage on this pin overrides the internal reference, it can be used for tracking and sequencing.

logical_pins.name: RT/CLK
logical_pins.description: Resistor timing and external clock. An internal amplifier holds this pin at a fixed voltage when using an external resistor to ground to set the switching frequency. If the pin is pulled above the PLL upper threshold, a mode change occurs and the pin becomes a synchronization input.

logical_pins.name: PWRGD
logical_pins.description: An open-drain output; asserts low if output voltage is low due to thermal shutdown, dropout, overvoltage or EN shutdown.

logical_pins.name: VSENSE
logical_pins.description: Inverting node of the transconductance (gm) error amplifier.

logical_pins.name: COMP
logical_pins.description: Error amplifier output, and input to the output switch current comparator. Connect frequency compensation components to this pin.

logical_pins.name: PH
logical_pins.description: The source of the internal high-side power MOSFET

logical_pins.name: Thermal pad
logical_pins.description: GND pin must be electrically connected to the thermal pad on the printed circuit board for proper operation.
